faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about fxTunnel.

items.q: What is fxTunnel?
items.a: fxTunnel is a reverse tunneling service that exposes your local servers to the internet via HTTP subdomains, TCP ports, or UDP ports. It works like ngrok but with a desktop GUI client, free custom subdomains, and no session timeouts.

items.q: What is the best free alternative to ngrok?
items.a: fxTunnel offers free custom subdomains, unlimited bandwidth, no session timeout, and a desktop GUI client — features that competitors like ngrok charge for. You can use any subdomain you want on the free plan, unlike ngrok which assigns random URLs.

items.q: What tunneling service has a desktop app?
items.a: fxTunnel is the only tunneling service with a native cross-platform desktop client. The GUI app runs on Windows, macOS, and Linux, letting you manage tunnels visually without using the terminal.

items.q: Does fxTunnel support UDP tunneling?
items.a: Yes. fxTunnel supports HTTP, TCP, and UDP protocols. UDP tunneling is useful for game servers, VoIP, DNS, and other real-time applications. Most competitors only support HTTP and TCP.

items.q: How do I test webhooks locally?
items.a: Run 'fxtunnel http 3000 --subdomain webhooks' to get a public URL like webhooks.fxtun.dev. Point your webhook provider (Stripe, GitHub, Twilio) to this URL, and all requests will be forwarded to your local server on port 3000.

items.q: Can I use custom domains with fxTunnel?
items.a: Yes. On paid plans you can bring your own domain with automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt. Set up a CNAME record for subdomains or an A record for apex domains, and fxTunnel handles SSL certificate provisioning automatically.

items.q: Does fxTunnel have bandwidth limits?
items.a: No. There are no bandwidth or request limits on any plan, including free. Your tunnels can handle as much traffic as needed without throttling or caps.

items.q: Is there a session timeout?
items.a: No. fxTunnel tunnels stay open indefinitely. Unlike ngrok's free tier which used to have 2-hour session limits, fxTunnel keeps your tunnels running 24/7 with automatic reconnection.

items.q: Can I self-host fxTunnel?
items.a: Yes. fxTunnel is fully self-hostable. You can run your own server on your infrastructure, giving you complete control over your tunneling setup, data, and security.

items.q: How much does fxTunnel cost?
items.a: fxTunnel has a free plan with 1 tunnel and any subdomain you want. Paid plans start at $2.50/month for more tunnels, reserved subdomains, custom domains, and the traffic inspector. There are no hidden fees.

items.q: What's the difference between fxTunnel and Cloudflare Tunnel?
items.a: Cloudflare Tunnel supports HTTP and TCP, but not UDP, and requires the Cloudflare ecosystem. fxTunnel supports HTTP, TCP, and UDP, has a desktop GUI, doesn't require any ecosystem lock-in, and is self-hostable. Cloudflare Tunnel is free but limited to their platform.

items.q: How do I expose localhost to the internet?
items.a: Install the fxTunnel client, then run 'fxtunnel http 3000' to instantly get a public URL for your local server running on port 3000. No configuration needed — just one command.

items.q: Does fxTunnel support HTTPS?
items.a: Yes. All HTTP tunnels automatically get HTTPS via the server's wildcard TLS certificate. Custom domains also get automatic HTTPS through Let's Encrypt certificate provisioning.

items.q: What is the traffic inspector?
items.a: The traffic inspector is a real-time HTTP request/response viewer built into fxTunnel. You can search, filter by method or status, and replay requests with one click — perfect for debugging webhooks and API integrations.

items.q: Does fxTunnel work with WebSockets?
items.a: Yes. fxTunnel provides full bidirectional WebSocket passthrough with no additional configuration. WebSocket connections are forwarded transparently to your local server.

about.title: About
about.projectText1: fxTunnel is an indie project created by a solo developer. The idea was born from frustration with existing reverse tunneling solutions: ngrok became too expensive and pivoted to an 'API gateway platform', Cloudflare Tunnel locks you into their ecosystem and doesn't support UDP, and Pinggy and serveo have session limitations. None of them offered a desktop application.
about.projectText2: fxTunnel solves these problems: free custom subdomains of your choice, zero request or bandwidth limits, no session timeouts, support for three protocols (HTTP, TCP, and UDP), and a native desktop application for Windows, macOS, and Linux. The project launched in 2025 and is actively developed.
about.missionText: Make reverse tunneling accessible, straightforward, and convenient for every developer. fxTunnel doesn't try to become a 'platform for everything' — it's a focused tool that does one thing and does it well. Minimal overhead allows us to offer a generous free tier and low prices on paid plans.
about.developerText1: The project is built and maintained by a solo developer — fxcode.ru. Specialization: Go, infrastructure, DevOps tools. Philosophy: build developer tools that solve real problems — no unnecessary complexity, no marketing buzzwords, no VC funding, no bloated teams.
about.techText: fxTunnel is built on a modern stack emphasizing performance, minimal dependencies, and easy deployment.

techItems.backend: Go 1.24+ — server and CLI client, single binary with no external dependencies
techItems.frontend: Vue 3 + TypeScript — web UI with SSG for SEO and fast loading
techItems.multiplexing: yamux — stream multiplexing over a single TCP connection for minimal latency
techItems.database: SQLite — lightweight embedded database, zero installation and configuration
techItems.desktop: Wails — native desktop app (Go + Vue3) for Windows, macOS, and Linux
techItems.security: TLS 1.3, Let's Encrypt — automatic HTTPS for all tunnels and custom domains

about.openSourceText: fxTunnel is an open-source project. The repository is available on GitHub: review the architecture, report bugs, suggest improvements, or deploy your own server. Contributions of any scale are welcome — from typo fixes to new features.

compare.heroSubtitle: Feature-by-feature comparison to help you choose the right tunneling tool.
compare.ctaSubtitle: Get started in under a minute. No credit card required.

sections.pricingTitle: How much does fxTunnel cost compared to ngrok?
sections.pricingText: fxTunnel offers a free tier with 1 tunnel and any custom subdomain you want. Paid plans start at just $2.50/month. ngrok's free tier gives you random URLs only, and the first paid plan costs $8/month — over 3x more expensive. For teams and businesses, the savings add up fast.
sections.subdomainsTitle: Can you use custom subdomains for free?
sections.subdomainsText: On fxTunnel's free plan, you can pick any subdomain you want — like myapp.fxtun.dev. ngrok assigns random URLs on the free plan, and custom subdomains require a paid subscription. No more sharing ugly random links with clients.
sections.limitsTitle: Are there request limits on fxTunnel vs ngrok?
sections.limitsText: fxTunnel has zero request or bandwidth limits on any plan, including free. ngrok limits free users to 20,000 requests per month and 1 GB bandwidth. For webhook testing, demo sharing, or API development, this can be restrictive.
sections.protocolsTitle: What protocols do fxTunnel and ngrok support?
sections.protocolsText: fxTunnel supports HTTP, TCP, and UDP tunnels. ngrok supports HTTP and TCP but has no UDP support. If you need to expose game servers, DNS services, VoIP, or any UDP-based application, fxTunnel is your only choice among managed tunneling services.
sections.guiTitle: Does fxTunnel have a desktop app?
sections.guiText: fxTunnel is the only tunneling service with a native desktop application. Manage tunnels visually, see connection status in the system tray, and enjoy auto-reconnect without touching the terminal. Perfect for developers who prefer a GUI workflow.
sections.inspectorTitle: How does fxTunnel's traffic inspector compare to ngrok?
sections.inspectorText: Both services offer traffic inspection, but fxTunnel makes it available from just $2.50/month with unlimited retention. ngrok charges $8/month and retains data for only 72 hours. The fxTunnel inspector supports real-time filtering, search, and one-click request replay.

faq.q: Can I migrate from ngrok to fxTunnel?
faq.a: Yes. Install the fxTunnel client, create an account, and replace your ngrok commands. The CLI syntax is similar: 'fxtunnel http 3000' instead of 'ngrok http 3000'. Your webhooks and integrations just need the new URL.

faq.q: Does fxTunnel work with the same tools as ngrok?
faq.a: Yes. fxTunnel works with any tool that uses webhooks or needs a public URL — Stripe, GitHub, Twilio, Slack, and more. Just point the webhook URL to your fxTunnel address.

faq.q: Is fxTunnel as reliable as ngrok?
faq.a: fxTunnel uses yamux multiplexing over TLS for reliable, low-latency connections. Auto-reconnect ensures your tunnels stay online 24/7. The Go-based server is lightweight and handles thousands of concurrent connections.

faq.q: Why is fxTunnel cheaper than ngrok?
faq.a: fxTunnel is built by a solo developer with minimal overhead. No VC funding, no bloated team — just a focused product that does one thing well. The savings are passed directly to users.

sections.pricingTitle: How much does fxTunnel cost compared to Cloudflare Tunnel?
sections.pricingText: Cloudflare Tunnel is free, but it requires a Cloudflare account and your domain must be managed through Cloudflare DNS. fxTunnel is also free to start, and you keep full independence — no ecosystem lock-in, no mandatory DNS migration. Use any domain registrar you prefer.
sections.protocolsTitle: What protocols do fxTunnel and Cloudflare Tunnel support?
sections.protocolsText: Cloudflare Tunnel supports HTTP and TCP traffic, but not UDP. fxTunnel supports all three protocols: HTTP, TCP, and UDP. Need to expose a game server, DNS, or another UDP service? Only fxTunnel handles UDP with dedicated port allocation.
sections.independenceTitle: Do you need Cloudflare to use fxTunnel?
sections.independenceText: With Cloudflare Tunnel, your domain DNS must be on Cloudflare, your traffic routes through their network, and you depend on their platform. fxTunnel is fully independent — use any DNS provider, any domain registrar, and optionally self-host for complete control.
sections.guiTitle: Do fxTunnel or Cloudflare Tunnel have a GUI?
sections.guiText: Cloudflare Tunnel requires the cloudflared CLI and dashboard configuration. fxTunnel offers a native desktop application with visual tunnel management, system tray integration, and auto-reconnect — no terminal or web dashboard needed.
sections.selfHostedText: fxTunnel can be deployed on your own infrastructure. Run your own tunneling server with SQLite, manage users, and keep all data on your servers. Cloudflare Tunnel has no self-hosted option — you always depend on Cloudflare's infrastructure.

faq.q: Why choose fxTunnel over Cloudflare Tunnel?
faq.a: If you need UDP tunneling, don't want to migrate DNS to Cloudflare, want a desktop GUI, or need traffic inspection — fxTunnel is the better choice. Cloudflare Tunnel supports HTTP and TCP but not UDP, and locks you into the Cloudflare ecosystem.

Yes, but with strings attached. Your domain must use Cloudflare DNS, you're locked into their ecosystem, and you only get HTTP and TCP support (no UDP). fxTunnel's free plan gives you HTTP, TCP, UDP, any subdomain, and no ecosystem requirements.

faq.q: Can fxTunnel handle the same traffic as Cloudflare?
faq.a: For development, staging, and small-to-medium production workloads — absolutely. fxTunnel has no bandwidth limits. For large enterprise deployments needing global CDN, Cloudflare may be more suitable, but fxTunnel can be self-hosted and scaled.

Yes. You can use fxTunnel with any domain, including those on Cloudflare DNS. Just point a CNAME record to your fxTunnel address. Unlike Cloudflare Tunnel, fxTunnel doesn't require Cloudflare DNS — it works with any provider.

extraFeatures.label: Extra features
extraFeatures.competitor: Secrets, webhooks, monitoring, bastion, password manager

sections.pricingTitle: How much does fxTunnel cost compared to tuna.am?
sections.pricingText: fxTunnel offers a free plan with 1 tunnel, any subdomain, and no time limits. tuna.am's free plan is limited to 30 minutes per session and only one HTTP tunnel with dynamic subdomains. tuna.am's paid "Pro" plan starts at 299 RUB/month, while fxTunnel's Starter plan is 200 RUB/month with 3 tunnels. tuna.am includes many extra services (secrets, webhooks, monitoring), but if you only need tunneling, fxTunnel is more affordable.
sections.protocolsTitle: What protocols do fxTunnel and tuna.am support?
sections.protocolsText: fxTunnel supports HTTP, TCP, and UDP tunnels. tuna.am supports HTTP, TCP, and SSHd tunnels but does not offer UDP. If you need to expose game servers, DNS services, or VoIP applications, fxTunnel is the better choice. tuna.am has a unique SSHd tunnel type that provides a built-in SSH server without requiring your own SSH daemon.
sections.limitsTitle: What are the free plan limits for fxTunnel vs tuna.am?
sections.limitsText: fxTunnel's free plan has no session timeout, no request limits, and no bandwidth caps. tuna.am's free "Novice" plan restricts sessions to 30 minutes, offers only 1 HTTP tunnel with dynamic subdomains, and does not include TCP support. For developers who need reliable, always-on tunnels for webhook testing or demo sharing, fxTunnel's free plan is significantly more practical.
sections.platformTitle: How do fxTunnel and tuna.am platforms compare?
sections.platformText: tuna.am positions itself as a full developer platform — beyond tunnels, it offers secret management, webhook inspection, bug reports, monitoring, SSH bastion, and a password manager. This is a real advantage if you need an all-in-one tool. fxTunnel focuses purely on tunneling — doing one thing and doing it well. If you need just tunnels with maximum flexibility and lower cost, fxTunnel is the focused choice.
sections.guiTitle: Do fxTunnel and tuna.am have desktop apps?
sections.guiText: Both services offer desktop GUI applications. fxTunnel's desktop app is built with Wails (Go + Vue3) and provides full tunnel management, system tray integration, and auto-reconnect. Tuna Desktop is also available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. This is one area where both services are ahead of most competitors like ngrok and Cloudflare Tunnel.
sections.selfHostedTitle: Can you self-host fxTunnel or tuna.am?
sections.selfHostedText: fxTunnel can be fully self-hosted on your own infrastructure — deploy the server, manage users, and keep all data under your control. tuna.am offers a self-hosted node option for enterprise customers by request, but it is not generally available. For teams that need complete data sovereignty, fxTunnel is the more accessible choice.

faq.q: Which is better for tunnels only — fxTunnel or tuna.am?
faq.a: If you only need tunneling, fxTunnel offers better value: lower prices, no session timeout on the free plan, UDP support, and self-hosting. tuna.am is better if you want an all-in-one platform with secrets management, monitoring, and SSH bastion in addition to tunnels.
No. tuna.am supports HTTP, TCP, and SSHd tunnels but not UDP. fxTunnel is one of the few services that supports all three protocols: HTTP, TCP, and UDP.

faq.q: Can I use both services for free?
faq.a: Yes. fxTunnel's free plan gives you 1 tunnel with any subdomain and no time limit. tuna.am's free plan gives you 1 HTTP tunnel limited to 30 minutes per session with a dynamic subdomain.

faq.q: Is tuna.am's platform worth the extra cost?
faq.a: If you actively need secret management, webhook capture, monitoring, and SSH bastion features, tuna.am's all-in-one approach can save you from managing multiple tools. If you just need reliable tunneling, fxTunnel delivers more for less.

sections.pricingTitle: How much does fxTunnel cost compared to xTunnel?
sections.pricingText: xTunnel offers a simple pricing model: free with limitations, 99 RUB/month for the Personal plan, and 299 RUB/month for Professional. fxTunnel's free plan already includes a permanent subdomain of your choice with no time limits. fxTunnel's Starter plan at 200 RUB/month gives you 3 tunnels with reserved subdomains, custom domains, and a traffic inspector — features xTunnel does not offer at any price.
sections.protocolsTitle: What protocols do fxTunnel and xTunnel support?
sections.protocolsText: fxTunnel supports HTTP, TCP, and UDP tunnels on all plans. xTunnel's free plan only supports HTTP; TCP requires a paid subscription. Neither the Personal nor Professional plan includes UDP support. If you need to expose game servers, DNS, or VoIP services, fxTunnel is the only option.
sections.limitsTitle: What are the limits on fxTunnel vs xTunnel?
sections.limitsText: xTunnel imposes strict limits: 100 requests per minute and 1 GB per day on the free plan, scaling to 1,000 requests per minute and 5 GB per day on Professional. fxTunnel has no request limits and no bandwidth caps on any plan, including free. For API development, webhook testing, or demo sharing, fxTunnel's unlimited approach means you never hit a wall.
sections.guiTitle: Does xTunnel have a desktop app?
sections.guiText: xTunnel is a CLI-only tool with no graphical interface. fxTunnel offers a native desktop application for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Manage tunnels visually, monitor connection status in the system tray, and enjoy auto-reconnect without opening a terminal. For developers who prefer a GUI workflow, fxTunnel is the clear winner.
sections.inspectorTitle: Do fxTunnel and xTunnel have traffic inspectors?
sections.inspectorText: xTunnel does not offer a traffic inspector. fxTunnel includes a built-in traffic inspector (from the Starter plan) that lets you view, filter, search, and replay HTTP requests in real time. For debugging webhooks, API integrations, and payment callbacks, the inspector saves hours of manual log analysis.
sections.selfHostedTitle: Can you self-host fxTunnel or xTunnel?
sections.selfHostedText: fxTunnel can be deployed on your own infrastructure with full user management and data control. xTunnel is a closed SaaS service with no self-hosted option. fxTunnel also offers reserved subdomains, custom domain support, and Let's Encrypt HTTPS — features not available on xTunnel.

faq.a: Yes, but with significant limitations: temporary public address (changes on each restart), HTTP only (no TCP), 100 requests per minute, and 1 GB per day bandwidth cap. fxTunnel's free plan has no such restrictions — you get a permanent subdomain of your choice with unlimited requests and bandwidth.

faq.q: Does xTunnel support custom subdomains or domains?
faq.a: No. xTunnel generates temporary or permanent public addresses but does not let you choose a custom subdomain or bring your own domain. fxTunnel lets you pick any subdomain for free (e.g., myapp.fxtun.dev) and supports custom domains from the Starter plan.

fxTunnel offers more features at every price point: custom subdomains (free), UDP support, desktop GUI, traffic inspector, self-hosting, no request limits, and no bandwidth caps. xTunnel is a simpler service suitable for basic HTTP tunnel needs.

faq.q: Can I migrate from xTunnel to fxTunnel?
faq.a: Yes. Install the fxTunnel client, create a free account, and use similar commands: 'fxtunnel http 3000' instead of 'xtunnel http 3000'. You immediately get a permanent custom subdomain with no request or bandwidth limits.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: We accept bank cards (Visa, Mastercard, MIR) via YooKassa for Russian users, and international cards via Creem for users outside Russia. Payment is monthly with optional auto-renewal.

items.q: Can I cancel my subscription anytime?
items.a: Yes. You can cancel at any time from your profile. Your paid features remain active until the end of the billing period. No cancellation fees.

items.q: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
items.a: There is no trial period, but the Free plan is available indefinitely. You can start with Free and upgrade when you need more tunnels, reserved subdomains, or the traffic inspector.

items.q: What happens if I downgrade my plan?
items.a: When you downgrade, the new plan takes effect at the end of your current billing period. Excess reserved subdomains and custom domains will be released automatically.

items.q: Do you offer refunds?
items.a: Yes. Full refund before the paid period starts, proportional refund after. Refunds are processed within 14 business days via the original payment method. Contact support@fxtun.ru.
